questionHtml: Suggest why the existence of salts containing an ion with this formula could be predicted. Refer to section 7 of the data booklet.
markschemeHtml:
same group as Mn «which forms MnO4-»
OR
in group 7/has 7 valence electrons, so its «highest» oxidation state is +7    [✔]

meta.title: Suggest why the existence of salts containing an ion with this formula could be predicted. Refer to section 7 of the data booklet.
breadcrumbLabels: Structure 3.1—The periodic table: Classification of elements

Structure 3.1.6—The oxidation state is a number assigned to an atom to show the number of electrons transferred in forming a bond. It is the charge that atom would have if the compound were composed of ions. Deduce the oxidation states of an atom in an ion or a compound.
Structure 3.1—The periodic table: Classification of elements
